跳转到导航跳跃搜索赞布拉克(波斯语：زمبورک‎)，字面意思是黄蜂，是早期现代的一种特殊形式的自行火炮，特点是从骆驼上的旋转架发射小型大炮。赞布拉克的操作者被称为赞布拉克。这种武器被火药帝国使用，特别是萨法维王朝、帖木儿帝国和阿夫沙里德王朝的伊朗帝国，因为伊朗高原崎岖，这使得典型的重型大炮运输成为问题。
赞布拉克在18世纪成为致命武器。普什图族人在古尔纳巴德战役中用它达到了致命的效果，击溃了数量上占优势的萨法维帝国军队。赞布拉克也被成功地运用在纳德的战役中，当时国王和军事天才纳德·沙阿利用赞布拉克兵团和常规大炮的常规炮兵部队，在许多战斗中产生了毁灭性的影响，如丹罕战役(1729年)、叶格夫ā路战役和卡纳尔战役。
赞布拉克可能是由最初将大弩安装在骆驼上的埃及战士开发的，直到19世纪和20世纪才迅速被阿拉伯人、普什图人、莫卧儿，特别是伊朗和印度军队采用。[需要引用]赞布拉克是卡贾尔军队中的皇家卫队之一。波斯赞布拉克兵团将由音乐家用巨大的骆驼架子鼓伴奏，以制造更多的噪音，给敌人留下深刻印象。赞布拉克在盎格鲁-阿富汗战争和盎格鲁-锡克战争中被用来对付入侵的英国人。
赞布拉克由一名骑在骆驼上的士兵组成，骑着一把装着旋转枪(一只小猎鹰)的士兵，它被铰链连接在从骆驼鞍上伸出的金属叉架上。为了打响大炮，骆驼必须双膝跪地。这个名字来源于波斯单词，意为黄蜂赞布(زنبور)，可能是参考了早期骆驼上制作的弩的声音。骆驼的机动性，再加上旋转枪的灵活性和强大的火力，构成了一支令人生畏的军事单位，尽管大炮的精确度和射程都相当低。轻型大炮对重型防御工事也不是特别有用。
准噶尔汗国在战斗中使用骆驼搭载的微型大炮。[1]：准噶尔-清战争期间，双方共部署枪支、大炮等火药武器89件。[1]：95。
^a b Millward，James A(2007)。欧亚十字路口：新疆史。哥伦比亚大学出版社。ISBN978-0-231231-13924-3。

Jump to navigation  Jump to search  Zamburak ( Persian:  زمبورک‎), literally meaning  wasp, was a specialized form of  self-propelled artillery from the  early modern period, featuring small cannons fired from swivel-mounts on camels. The operator of a zamburak is known as a  zamburakchi. The weapon was used by the  gunpowder empires, especially the Iranian empires of the  Safavid dynasty,  Timurid Empire and  Afsharid dynasty, due to the ruggedness of the  Iranian Plateau, which made typical transportation of heavy cannons problematic.
 The zamburak became a deadly weapon in the 18th century. The  Pashtuns used it to deadly effect in the  Battle of Gulnabad, routing a numerically superior imperial Safavid army. The zamburak was also used successfully in  Nader's Campaigns, when the shah and military genius  Nader Shah utilized a zamburak corps in conjunction with a regular artillery corps of conventional cannon to devastating effect in numerous battles such as at the  Battle of Damghan (1729), the  Battle of Yeghevārd, and the  Battle of Karnal.
   Probably developed by  Egyptian warriors who originally mounted large crossbows on camels, the zamburak was rapidly adopted by  Arabs, Pashtuns,  Mughal and especially Iranian and  Indian armies up until the 19th and 20th century. [   citation needed] Zamburaks were one of the royal guard units in the Qajar Army. A Persian zamburak regiment would be accompanied by musicians with huge camel-mounted drums, in order to create even more noise and impress the enemy. Zamburaks were used against the invading  British in the  Anglo-Afghan Wars and in the  Anglo-Sikh wars.
 A zamburak consisted of a soldier on a  camel with a mounted  swivel gun (a small  falconet), which was hinged on a metal fork-rest protruding from the saddle of the animal. In order to fire the cannon, the camel would be put on its knees. The name is derived from the  Persian word for wasp  zambur (زنبور), possibly in reference to the sound earlier camel-mounted crossbows made. The mobility of the camel combined with the flexibility and heavy firepower of the swivel gun made for an intimidating military unit, although the accuracy and range of the cannon was rather low. The light cannon was also not particularly useful against heavy fortifications.
 The  Dzungar Khanate used camel-mounted miniature cannons in battle.  [1] : 89 Gunpowder weapons like guns and cannons were deployed by both sides during the  Dzungar–Qing Wars.  [1] : 95
   ^     a     b   Millward, James A (2007).   Eurasian Crossroads: A History of Xinjiang. Columbia University Press.  ISBN   978-0-231-13924-3.
title: 赞布拉克

note:
据知情人士透露，软银集团(SoftBank Group)正在探索其他选择，包括全部或部分出售或公开发行英国芯片设计公司ARM Holdings，这家日本企业集团四年前以320亿美元收购了ARM Holdings。
知情人士说，由高盛集团(Goldman Sachs Group)担任顾问的这项评估还处于早期阶段。目前尚不清楚金融或行业参与者可能会对ARM产生多大兴趣，软银也有可能最终选择什么都不做。
软银此前曾表示，可能会在某个时候将ARM重返公开市场。然而，随着软银寻求从其各种稳定的资产中筹集现金，以安抚维权投资者埃利奥特管理公司(Elliott Management Corp.)，这样的举措变得更加紧迫。埃利奥特管理公司一直在鼓动公司进行改革。
软银曾表示，计划出售至多410亿美元的资产，以支撑其苦苦挣扎的投资组合，并回购自己的股票，这些股票的交易价格相对于资产净值有很大折扣。软银有一大袋资产可供选择；除了ARM和最近出售的价值约200亿美元的T-Mobile美国股票外，软银还持有中国电商巨头阿里巴巴集团控股公司(Alibaba Group Holding)和一家领先的日本手机供应商的大量股份。
软银在2016年收购了ARM，该公司设计为全球大部分智能手机提供动力的微处理器。当时，这是软银有史以来最大的一笔收购。
软银首席执行官孙正义(Masayoshi Son)称赞此次收购是该公司的“范式转变”，使其能够利用物联网的潜力，物联网指的是日常设备的连接。但ARM开发的用于管理联网设备的软件的销售相对持平，不包括收购带来的提振。
ARM上周表示，计划将两个物联网服务部门转移到新的实体中，这些实体将由软银拥有和运营，这是专注于其核心半导体IP业务的举措的一部分。该公司表示，如果获得批准，预计转移将在9月底之前敲定。
软银规模1,000亿美元的Vision Fund投资于科技公司，持有ARM 25%的股权，该基金过去曾考虑将股权转回软银，因为基金高管认为这家科技公司营收增长乏善可陈，拖累了其投资组合的整体估值。
最近，远景基金(Vision Fund)的巨额亏损打击了软银的收益，破坏了筹集第二个大型投资工具的计划。
近年来，芯片行业一直是交易活动的可靠来源，因为企业将自己定位为支持汽车和工业行业的发展以及智能设备的激增。周一，ADI同意以约200亿美元收购Maxim Integrated Products，这笔交易将缔造一家专门生产用于电源管理的模拟半导体的公司，可以更好地与行业巨头德州仪器(Texas Instruments)竞争
note_en:
SoftBank Group    9984  4.23%  is exploring alternatives including a full or partial sale or public offering of British chip designer Arm Holdings, which the Japanese conglomerate bought four years ago for $32 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.
  The review, on which  Goldman Sachs Group  is advising, is at an early stage, the people said. It isn’t known how much interest financial or industry players might have in Arm, and it is possible SoftBank will ultimately choose to do nothing.
  SoftBank has previously indicated it could return Arm to public markets at some point. Such a move has gained urgency, however, as SoftBank seeks to raise cash from its varied stable of assets to mollify activist investor Elliott Management Corp., which has been agitating for changes at the company.
  SoftBank has said it plans to sell up to $41 billion in assets to prop up its struggling portfolio and buy back its own shares, which trade at a steep discount relative to net asset value. It has a grab bag of assets to choose from; in addition to Arm and roughly $20 billion worth of  T-Mobile US  shares it recently sold, SoftBank also owns large stakes in Chinese e-commerce giant  Alibaba Group Holding  and a leading Japanese cellphone provider.
    SoftBank bought Arm, which designs microprocessors that power most of the world’s smartphones, in 2016. At the time it was SoftBank’s largest-ever acquisition.
  SoftBank chief Masayoshi Son hailed the acquisition as a “paradigm shift” at the company, enabling it to take advantage of the potential of the Internet of Things, which refers to the connectivity of everyday devices. But sales of the software that Arm developed for managing connected devices have been relatively flat, excluding a boost from acquisitions.
  Arm last week said it planned to transfer two IoT-services units into new entities that would be owned and operated by SoftBank as part of a move to focus on its core semiconductor-IP business. The company said it expected the transfer, if approved, to be finalized by the end of September.
  SoftBank’s $100 billion Vision Fund, which invests in tech companies and holds a 25% stake in Arm, has in the past considered transferring the stake back to SoftBank because fund executives believe the tech company’s lackluster revenue growth has been a drag on the overall valuation of its portfolio.
  SoftBank’s earnings have been battered recently by  huge losses at the Vision Fund, undermining plans to raise a second big investment vehicle.
  The chip sector has been a reliable source of deal activity in recent years as companies position themselves to support the evolution of the auto and industrial sectors and the proliferation of smart devices. On Monday,  Analog Devices  agreed to buy  Maxim Integrated Products  for roughly $20 billion in  a deal that would create a company specializing in analog semiconductors used in power management that could better compete with industry giant  Texas Instruments
title: 消息人士：软银正在探索全部/部分出售或公开发行ARM；软银四年前以320亿美元收购ARM
title_en: Sources: SoftBank is exploring a full/partial sale or public offering of Arm; SoftBank bought Arm for $32B four years ago

note:
想象一个已经解决了人口过剩和环境崩溃问题的社会。犯罪不是问题，无家可归和饥饿也不是问题。种族主义？性别歧视？同性恋恐惧症？整理好了。科学已经征服了疾病和残疾。每个人都有有用的工作，完美的皮肤，完全的情绪平衡。每一天都是一种享受。每晚都是一个派对。
答：这是NBC流媒体服务“孔雀”(Peacock)的旗舰剧集“美丽新世界”(Brave New World)的社会科学背景。所有9集都将于周三上线。根据奥尔德斯·赫胥黎(Aldous Huxley)1932年关于自由爱情和社会控制的令人震惊的先见之明的小说改编而成，这是一个伪装成乌托邦的反乌托邦。或者反之亦然。
“这看起来很完美，”杰西卡·布朗·芬德利(Jessica Brown Findlay)说，她饰演遗传学家列尼娜·克朗。“但当你触及表面的那一刻，你就开始发现一些东西。”
“不过，是的，在那里待几天吧？”她补充说。“那就太好了。”
有声望的电视喜欢对未来的一瞥，而那些未来通常是黑暗的：“西部世界”、“黑镜”、“女仆的故事”。但是，大多数观众会模糊地记得-如果有的话-从一些高中或大学的教学大纲，“美丽的新世界”，提出了一个更加矛盾的前景和特殊的挑战。
这里有一个问题：你如何看待一部有近90年历史的小说，一个如此死板的文学水晶球，以至于它的许多预测(化学节育、情绪稳定剂、基因工程)已经成为现实，而且仍然让它感觉像是未来？
环球内容制作公司(Universal Content Productions)获得了这部小说的版权，安布林娱乐公司(Amblin Entertainment)为他们打造世界的印章带来了合作，“美丽的新世界”(Brave New World)从Syfy开始，然后搬到美国，最后登陆孔雀，沿途摆脱了故事和概念，偶尔还有作家。
环球内容制作公司(Universal Content Productions)总裁道恩·奥姆斯特德(Dawn Olmstead)说，“当你拥有大的IP时，通常会发生什么，你会一直摇摆，直到你把它做好。”
节目制作人大卫·维纳(David Wiener，“返校节”、“害怕行尸走肉”)显然有解决方案，他把社会理论放在三角恋中。他解释的理论部分是这样的：“赫胥黎，”他说，“非常害怕这样一个世界，在这个世界里，人们会变得如此性刺激，如此药理麻木，如此分心于娱乐和媒体，以至于他们无法以不舒服的方式审视自己内部和外部。”那么未来就是现在？
至于三角恋：列尼娜是一位喜欢拉链穿迷你裙的科学家，她住在新伦敦，这是一个已经消除了所有社会弊病的城邦。家庭、隐私和社会流动性也被抛弃了。当不舒服的感觉出现时，公民们会突袭索马-认为是精神上的彩虹彩虹-并用药物将它们带走。
渴望度假的列尼娜陪着伯纳德·马克思(哈里·劳埃德饰)，一位喜欢高领毛衣的行政人员，愉快地游览了野蛮之地。作为上流社会生活的历史公园，野蛮人土地提供基于过时习俗的剧本-婚姻，消费主义。我有没有提到过，新伦敦的每个人都带着简陋的英国口音，而野蛮之地的方言则是严格意义上的美国口音？
当假期出了问题-野蛮之地出现了煽动问题-伯纳德和列尼娜在汗流浃背、结结巴巴的约翰(奥尔登·埃伦赖克饰)和他粗暴的深色金发母亲(黛米·摩尔饰)的帮助下逃脱了。约翰和他们一起回到新伦敦，他、列尼娜和伯纳德，他们每个人都渴望更多的人类联系，形成了基本的几何学。(猜不出她会选择谁，但这里给你一个提示：他的基因仍然编码着体毛。)。
为了设想新伦敦和野蛮之地之间的光学接口(在新伦敦，每个人都通过仿生隐形眼镜接入互联网)，该剧聘请了制作设计师大卫·李(David Lee，“守望者”)。“赫胥黎的世界，我的意思是，这是一个令人难以置信的设计机会，”李说。
他说，他和他的团队希望避免其他电影和系列片的外观，尽管他们确实提到了“银翼杀手”(Blade Runner)的规模，以及“加塔卡”(Gattaca)的流畅现代主义。李在很大程度上看重野兽主义--奥斯卡·尼迈耶的“巴西利亚”，卡洛·斯卡帕的优雅室内装潢，还有伸展到天空的苏联纪念碑。
新伦敦的建筑由缓缓弯曲的混凝土建造，根据光线的不同，混凝土可以发出温暖或凉爽的光，新伦敦的建筑看起来既诱人又坚韧，就像勒·柯布西耶(Le Corbusier)的工作室(以一种好的方式)接受了高端水疗中心的委托。野蛮地带的视觉参照：拖车公园和腐烂的沃尔玛。
虽然比你想象的要少，但涉及的C.G.I.很多。“野蛮人的土地”是在英国邓吉内斯拍摄的。李兴高采烈地将发现的地点描述为一片“平坦、贫瘠的荒地”，只需要穿上套装。在威尔士加的夫郊外的龙工作室(Dragon Studios)，建筑规模超过了建筑规模，溢出到后方，然后又延伸到另一间工作室，总共有38套不同的布景和数十万平方英尺(约合3万平方米)的面积。布朗·芬德利(Brown Findlay)记得第一次走进Dragon Studios，发现“几乎就是这座巨大的混凝土教堂”，她说。
这些服装，至少有一千套，也区分了现代主义和未来主义。赫胥黎痴迷于拉链，服装设计师苏西·库特哈德(Susie Coulthard)对此表示敬意。但她将这些拉链嵌入到先锋材料中，与瑞士纺织品创新者雅各布·施莱普费尔(Jakob Schlaepfer)合作，开发出具有油性或玻璃外观的面料。有几套衣服是用气球乳胶做的。
布朗·芬德利(Brown Findlay)是“唐顿庄园”(Downton Abbey)和“哈洛特”(Harlot)等古装剧的老手，他说：“我穿的衣服让我觉得自己完全沉浸在自己正在做的事情中。”“当你穿了很多巨大的紧身连衣裙时，这很能说明问题。”
赫胥黎的技术需要更新换代的不仅仅是纺织品，主要是因为他想象中的很多东西(视频会议、电视、试管婴儿)已经实现了。就连他的自动驾驶飞机也在研发中。
这部小说的乌托邦景象，带有丑陋的种族主义和厌女症，也需要翻新。“这本书有很大的问题，”维纳说。因此，这部剧转向了平等、种族倒退和几个配角的性别颠倒。
“事实证明，这些角色并不一定需要白人或男性，”维纳说。
主人公也发生了一些变化。列尼娜，书中一个开朗的性爱兔子，已经被授予内部职位。浮夸的伯纳德已经软化了。在小说中，约翰是一个拘谨、极度神经质、反性和反娱乐的人。
“这有点像带迈克·彭斯去新伦敦，”维纳打趣地说。“没人会想看的。”
因此，埃伦雷奇饰演的约翰已经放松了，肌肉发达了，尽管他仍然非常情绪化。Ehrenreich在“独唱”中最出名的角色是名义上的虚张声势，他更喜欢把约翰描述成一个浪漫的人。
他说：“他唯一需要坚持的就是他对深沉、动人的爱情的热切信念。”
如果这部小说以讽刺为主，那么这里的基调就更矛盾了。它可以有它的乱交-毕竟，没有偶尔的狂欢，什么是有声望的电视？-也要对此说教。
也许你会有同样的矛盾心态。毕竟，一个以快乐为中心的社会--无关紧要的性行为、派对毒品、可再生的时尚--听起来并不可怕。Ehrenreich说：“在片场，我们肯定有过这样的时刻，这是相当不错的。”
但很可能那只是索玛说的道具。这部小说更具尖锐性和更具共鸣的一点是，娱乐是它自己的药物，让我们麻木，不会感到不适。饰演伯纳德的劳埃德说：“这是我们今天绝对认识到的。”“当你独自坐在黑暗中质疑这一切是怎么回事的那一刻，你的手机上有那么多事情要做。”
“美丽的新世界”-即将在孔雀应用程序上推出！-就是其中之一。是的，它传播哲学，思考“幸福和自由之间的关系，以及你们愿意为对方冒什么风险，”劳埃德说。
但正如环球内容总裁奥姆斯特德两次告诉我的那样，这个节目不应该感觉像家庭作业。当你看着建筑物、衣服、健美的身体、闪闪发光的皮肤时，你会做多少思考？性和社会批判到底融合到什么程度？维纳希望，多个赛季都足够好。
赫胥黎没有预见到这个确切的时刻。(我们中很少有人既有全球流行病，也没有人对2020年宾果卡进行了逾期的种族清算。)。但是，每个口袋里都有一台电脑，每种情绪都有社交媒体馈送，娱乐点播，生活就像直播一样，这一想法不会让他感到惊讶。
也许他现在甚至可以想象我们，拿起孔雀，点击一集又一集的“美丽新世界”。在世界燃烧的时候大吃大喝。
note_en:
Imagine a society that has solved the problems of overpopulation and environmental collapse. Crime is a nonissue, as are homelessness and hunger. Racism? Sexism? Homophobia? Sorted. Science has conquered disease and disability. Everyone has useful work, perfect skin, total emotional equilibrium. Every day is a pleasure. Every night is a party.
  Answer: It’s the social science backdrop for “Brave New World,” the flagship drama from  Peacock, NBC’s streaming service. All nine episodes are available on Wednesday. Based on Aldous Huxley’s alarmingly  prescient 1932 novel of free love and social control, it’s a dystopia dressed up as a utopia. Or vice versa.
 “It seems perfect,” said  Jessica Brown Findlay, who plays the geneticist Lenina Crowne. “But the minute you scratch the surface, you start to discover stuff.”
 “But yeah, a couple of days there?” she added. “That would be great.”
 Prestige television likes its glimpses of the future and those futures usually skew dark:  “Westworld,”  “Black Mirror,”  “The Handmaid’s Tale.” But “Brave New World,” which most viewers will remember — vaguely if at all — from some high school or college syllabus, presents a more ambivalent prospect and particular challenges.
 Here’s one: How do you take a nearly 90-year-old novel, a literary crystal ball so dead-on that many of its predictions (chemical birth control, mood stabilizers, genetic engineering) have already come true, and still make it feel like the future?
 A collaboration between  Universal Content Productions, which acquired the rights to the novel, and  Amblin Entertainment, brought on for their world-building chops, “Brave New World” began at Syfy, then moved to  USA, before landing at Peacock, shedding story and concept and the occasional writer along the way.
 “What often happens when you have big IP, you keep swinging until you get it right,” said Dawn Olmstead, the president of Universal Content Productions.
 The showrunner  David Wiener (“Homecoming,” “Fear the Walking Dead”) apparently had the solution, situating the social theory within a love triangle. The theory part he explained this way: “Huxley,” he said, “was very afraid of a world in which people would become so sexually stimulated, so pharmacologically numb and so distracted by entertainment and media, that they would fail to look within and beyond themselves in uncomfortable ways.” So the future is now?
 As for the love triangle: Lenina, a scientist inclined to zip-up minidresses, lives in New London, a city-state that has eliminated all social ills. Family, privacy and social mobility have been jettisoned, too. When uncomfortable feelings arise, citizens pop soma — think psychoactive Skittles — and drug them away.
  Eager for a holiday, Lenina accompanies Bernard Marx (Harry Lloyd), an administrator with a thing for turtlenecks, on a pleasure tour of the Savage Lands. A living history park for the upper classes, the Savage Lands offer playlets based on antiquated customs — marriage, consumerism. Did I mention that everyone in New London speaks in clipped British accents while the Savage Lands dialect is strictly American?
 When the holiday goes wrong — the Savage Lands has a sedition issue — Bernard and Lenina escape with the help of a sweaty, stubbly John ( Alden Ehrenreich) and his raspy, bottle-blond mom ( Demi Moore). John returns with them to New London and he, Lenina and Bernard, each of them grasping for greater human connection, form the basic geometry. (No prize for guessing who she chooses, but here’s a hint: His genes still encode body hair.)
 To envision New London and the Savage Lands and optical interfaces between (in New London, everyone plugs into the internet via biomorphic contact lenses), the show hired the production designer David Lee (“Watchmen”). “Huxley’s world, I mean, it’s a design opportunity beyond belief,” Lee said.
 He and his team wanted to avoid the look of other films and series, though they did reference “Blade Runner” for its scale, he said, and “Gattaca” for its sleek modernism. Mostly, Lee looked to Brutalism —  Oscar Niemeyer’s Brasília,  Carlo Scarpa’s elegant interiors, Soviet monuments that stretch heavenward.
 Built of gently curved concrete, which can glow warm or cool depending on the lighting, New London’s buildings look both seductive and unyielding, as though Le Corbusier’s studio had taken on a commission for a high-end spa (in a good way). The visual references for the Savage Lands: trailer parks and decayed Walmarts.
 There’s plenty of C.G.I. involved, though less than you might think. The Savage Lands were shot in Dungeness, England. Lee cheerfully described the found location as a “flat, barren wasteland” that needed only set dressing. At  Dragon Studios, just outside Cardiff, Wales, construction outgrew the buildings and spilled into the backlot and then into another studio, totaling 38 distinct sets and hundreds of thousands of square feet. Brown Findlay remembered stepping into Dragon Studios for the first time and finding “this great big concrete church almost,” she said.
  The costumes, at least a thousand of them, also split the difference between modernism and futurism. Huxley had an obsession with zippers, and the costume designer Susie Coulthard honored it. But she embedded those zippers in vanguard materials, partnering with the Swiss textile innovators  Jakob Schlaepfer on fabrics that have an oily or glassine appearance. A few outfits are made of balloon latex.
 “I felt so immersed in what I was doing by what I was wearing,” Brown Findlay, a veteran of period shows like “Downton Abbey” and “Harlots” said. “That’s saying something when you’ve worn a lot of huge giant corseted dresses.”
 Huxley’s technology needed updates even beyond textiles, mostly because a lot of what he imagined (videoconferencing, television, test-tube babies) has already come to pass. Even his self-driving aircraft are in the works.
 The novel’s utopian vision, with its ugly flares of racism and misogyny, also required renovation. “The book’s hugely problematic,” Wiener said. So the show pivoted toward equality, race-bending and gender-flipping several of the supporting characters.
 “It turns out there’s nothing about those characters that necessarily needed to be white or male,” Wiener said.
 The main characters have undergone some changes, too. Lenina, a cheerful sex bunny in the book, has been granted interiority. Pompous Bernard has softened. In the novel, John is prissy and deeply neurotic, anti-sex and anti-fun.
 “It’d be a little like taking Mike Pence to New London,” Wiener quipped. “No one would want to watch that.”
 So Ehrenreich’s John has loosened up and muscled up, though he remains extremely emo. Ehrenreich, best known as the titular swashbuckler in “Solo,” prefers to describe John as romantic.
 “The only thing he has to hold onto in is his ardent belief in a deep, emotional love,” he said.
 If the novel traffics mostly in satire, the tone here is more ambivalent. It gets to have its promiscuity — what is prestige TV, after all, without the occasional orgy? — and moralize about it, too.
 Maybe you will share that ambivalence. After all, a society organized around pleasure — consequence-free sex, party drugs, renewable fashion — doesn’t sound  terrible. “There were definitely moments on set where we were like, this is pretty good,” Ehrenreich said.
 But possibly that’s just the prop soma talking. One of the novel’s spikier and more resonant points is that entertainment is its own drug, numbing us against discomfort. “That’s something that we definitely recognize today,” Lloyd, who plays Bernard, said. “The moment where you sit on your own in the darkness to question what it’s all about, there’s so many things to do on your phone.”
 “Brave New World” — available soon on the Peacock app! — is one more of them. Yes, it traffics in philosophy and muses upon “the relationship between happiness and freedom, and what you’re willing to risk of each for the other,” Lloyd said.
 But as Olmstead, the Universal Content president, told me — twice — the show shouldn’t feel like homework. How much thinking will you do while you watch the buildings, the clothes, the toned bodies, the glowing skin? How well do sex and social critique actually mix? Well enough for more seasons, Wiener hopes.
 Huxley didn’t foresee this exact moment. (Very few of us had both a global pandemic and an overdue racial reckoning on our 2020 bingo card.) But the idea of a computer in every pocket, a social media feed for every mood, entertainment on demand and life as livestream wouldn’t have surprised him.
 Perhaps he can even imagine us now, pulling up Peacock, clicking on one “Brave New World” episode and then another. Bingeing while the world burns.
title: 评论：“勇敢的新世界”即将到来
title_en: Review: ‘Brave New World’ Arrives in the Future It Predicted

note:
在新冠肺炎疫情爆发之际，美国儿科学会澄清了其对学校重新开学的立场，此前特朗普政府多次利用该学会之前的声明向学校系统施压，要求其在秋季恢复面对面学习。
AAP在一份与三个大型教育组织的联合声明中强调，学校重新开学应该以科学和安全为依据--“而不是政治”。它还直接回应了特朗普总统拒绝向没有重新开学的学校提供资金的威胁，称此举是一种“误导性的做法”。
周一，世界卫生组织(World Health Organization)的传染病专家迈克尔·瑞安(Michael Ryan)呼应了这一观点，他恳求各国不要让学校重新开学成为“又一个政治足球”。
世卫组织卫生紧急情况项目执行主任瑞安在新闻发布会上说，“重新开学的最好、最安全的方式是在社区传播率低的背景下，而这种传播已经被广泛的综合战略有效地压制了下来。”瑞安是世卫组织卫生紧急情况项目的执行主任，他在一次新闻发布会上说。
尽管孩子们在新冠肺炎传播中扮演的角色仍然鲜为人知，但瑞安指出，学校在社区中错综复杂地交织在一起。他警告说，各国不能指望通过几周内专注于学校重新开学，然后转移到工作场所重新开学，然后转移到医疗机构的爆发等方式来有效地应对这一流行病。瑞安说，这是在玩“打鼹鼠”游戏。取而代之的是，各国应该把重点放在同时处理所有背景的全面、长期战略上。
“我们必须一边嚼口香糖一边走路，”他说。“我们必须根据我们孩子的最大利益做出决定。”
最近几天，特朗普政府加大了推动学校重新开学的力度，尽管美国大部分地区的案件出现了令人沮丧的激增。例如，佛罗里达州仅在周日就报告了创纪录的超过1.5万例病例，是自大流行开始以来任何一个州中最大的。
世卫组织总干事特德罗斯·阿达诺姆·盖布赖斯(Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus)周一对美国和其他出现激增的国家发出了可怕的警告。“情况会越来越糟，”他说，除非人们开始遵循防止疾病传播的基本预防措施。即身体距离、洗手、戴口罩、咳嗽礼仪、生病时呆在家里。
尽管如此，特朗普政府仍将学校重新开学视为重振经济的关键，并加大了对各州的压力，要求它们在今年秋天，也就是大选前开放物理教室。在其理论基础上，政府经常引用AAP 6月份的临时指导意见，该指导意见“强烈主张，下一学年的所有政策考虑都应该从让学生亲自到校的目标开始。”该学院指出，学校为儿童提供了许多好处，例如除了学术指导外，还为孩子们提供了“社交和情感技能、安全性、可靠的营养、身体/语言和心理健康治疗，以及体育活动的机会”等其他好处。
但AAP一直表示，任何回报都应该安全进行，需要更多的教育投资-所有这些都在周五发布的新声明中强调。
AAP与美国教师联合会(AFT)、国家教育协会(NEA)和学校主管协会(AASA)一起写道：“重返校园对儿童的健康发展和福祉很重要，但我们必须以对所有学生、教师和教职员工都安全的方式追求重新开学。科学应该推动安全重新开放学校的决策。公共卫生机构必须根据证据提出建议，而不是基于政治。“。
这些团体呼吁国会和政府为学校安全重新开学提供更多资金，并警告说，任何位于新冠肺炎社区传播水平较高的地区的学校“都不应该被强制重新开学”。
note_en:
The American Academy of Pediatrics has clarified its stance on school reopening amid the COVID-19 pandemic after the Trump administration repeatedly used the academy’s previous statement to  pressure school systems to resume in-person learning in the fall.
 The AAP, in a joint statement with three large education organizations, emphasized that school reopening should be informed by science and safety—“not politics.” It also directly responded to a President Trump’s threat of withholding funding from schools who did not reopen, calling the move a “misguided approach.”
 The point was echoed Monday by Michael Ryan, an infectious disease expert with the World Health Organization, who implored countries not to let school reopening become a “yet another political football.”
 “The best and safest way to reopen schools is in the context of low community transmission that has been effectively suppressed by a broad-based comprehensive strategy,” Ryan, executive director of WHO’s health emergencies program,  said in a press briefing.
 Though the role children play in the spread of COVID-19 is still poorly understood, Ryan noted that schools are intricately entwined in communities. Countries can’t expect to effectively address the pandemic by focusing on school reopening for a few weeks, then move to workplace reopening, and then to outbreaks in healthcare setting, etc., he cautioned. That’s playing “whack-a-mole,” Ryan said. Instead, countries should focus on comprehensive, long-term strategies that address all contexts at once.
 “We’ve got to chew gum and walk at the same time,” he said. “And we have to make decisions based on the best interests of our children.”
  The Trump administration has intensified its push for schools to reopen in recent days despite dismaying surges in cases in much of the United States. Florida, for instance, reported a record-smashing tally of more than 15,000 cases on Sunday alone, the largest of any single state since the pandemic began.
 WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus on Monday gave a dire warning to the US and other countries seeing surges. “ It’s going to get worse and worse and worse,” he said, unless people started following the basic precautions against disease spread. That is, physical distancing, hand-washing, wearing masks, coughing etiquette, and staying at home when sick.
 Still, the Trump administration has eyed school reopening as key to reviving the economy and has stepped up pressure on states to have physical classrooms open this fall, before the election. In its rationale, the administration has often cited the AAP’s  June interim guidance, which “strongly advocates that all policy considerations for the coming school year should start with a goal of having students physically present in school.” The academy noted the many benefits schools provide children, such as “social and emotional skills, safety, reliable nutrition, physical/speech and mental health therapy, and opportunities for physical activity, among other benefits” beyond academic instruction.
 But the AAP has always said that any returns should be done safely and required more investment in education—all points emphasized in the new statement released Friday.
 Along with the American Federation of Teachers (AFT), National Education Association (NEA), and, AASA (the School Superintendents Association), the AAP wrote that “Returning to school is important for the healthy development and well-being of children, but we must pursue re-opening in a way that is safe for all students, teachers and staff. Science should drive decision-making on safely reopening schools. Public health agencies must make recommendations based on evidence, not politics.”
 The groups called on Congress and the administration to provide more funds for schools to safely reopen, and they cautioned that any schools in areas with high levels of COVID-19 community spread “should not be compelled to reopen.”
title: 世界卫生组织发出可怕警告，儿科医生走回学校重新开学的立场
title_en: Pediatricians walk back school-reopening stance as WHO gives dire warning

note:
一名男子在业余时间开发了一款应用程序，突出显示Wetherspoons场馆附近的独立酒吧，他说，人们的反应让他感到惊讶。
Android应用Neverspoons在发布的第一周就被下载了近1.8万次，超过了Shane Jones在前六个月的预期。
这个名字是酒吧连锁店Wetherspoons的双关语，但琼斯先生说，他只是想给较小的酒吧一个提振。
琼斯承认，其他几家连锁酒吧已经悄悄进入了这款应用，他表示，他计划清除那些向他投诉过的人，包括一家生菜和鼻涕鱼连锁店，以及几家Greene King和Firkin特许经营店。
到目前为止，琼斯先生已经上传了英国各地2500家酒吧的详细信息，还有971家酒吧是由饮酒者和房东提交的。
琼斯先生现在正和他的朋友奈德·保尔特一起开发这款应用程序，他正试图通过在网上查找这些照片来审查所有这些照片-他不能亲自探望任何人，因为他有潜在的健康状况，需要他进行防护。
他说：整个前提是，Wetherspoons是一家庞大的连锁店，他们不需要那么多帮助。
琼斯先生的灵感来自于参观了他家附近的一个村庄-曼彻斯特和利物浦之间的牛顿勒柳斯-在封锁之前，看到了很多关门的酒吧。
2018年，真正的麦片(Camra)运动警告说，英国每周都有18家酒馆倒闭，自大流行之前以来，酒吧行业面临着更艰难的时期。
Wetherspoon在英国和爱尔兰共和国拥有875家酒吧和58家酒店，约有4.4万名员工。
创始人蒂姆·马丁(Tim Martin)在封锁开始时表示，休假的员工可能无法按时拿到工资，并建议他们在其他地方工作，这引发了争议。
Neverspoons是免费下载的，目前只能在Android手机上使用。
它的工作方式是向用户显示最近的Wetherspoons在哪里，然后显示它周围的独立酒吧。
琼斯先生说，他已经通过在上面投放广告赚了一点钱，并从支持者那里收到了200英镑的捐款。
note_en:
A man who built an app in his spare time that highlights independent pubs close to Wetherspoons venues, says he has been amazed by the response.
 The Android app Neverspoons has been downloaded nearly 18,000 times in its first week - more than Shane Jones expected from the first six months.
  The name is a pun on the pub chain Wetherspoons but Mr Jones said he just wanted to give smaller pubs a boost.
 Mr Jones admitted that "a handful" of other chain pub venues had already crept on to the app and he said he planned to "weed out" those people had complained to him about - including one from the Slug and Lettuce chain and a few Greene King and Firkin franchises.
   Mr Jones has uploaded details of 2,500 pubs from around the UK so far and a further 971 have been submitted by drinkers and landlords.
  Mr Jones, who is now working on the app with his friend, Ned Poulter, is attempting to vet them all by looking them up online - he is unable to visit any personally as he has an underlying health condition that requires him to shield.
 He said: "The whole premise is, Wetherspoons is a massive chain and they don't need as much help."
 Mr Jones was inspired after visiting a village near his home in Newton Le Willows - midway between Manchester and Liverpool - before lockdown and seeing lots of shuttered pubs.
 In 2018 The Campaign for Real Ale (Camra) warned that  18 drinking establishments in the UK were going out of business every week and the pub trade has faced even more difficult times since before the pandemic.
 Wetherspoon has 875 pubs and 58 hotels in the UK and Republic of Ireland and about 44,000 employees.
 Founder Tim Martin caused controversy at the start of lockdown  when he said furloughed staff might not be paid on time and suggested they took a job elsewhere.
   Neverspoons is free to download and is available only on Android phones at the moment.
 It works by showing the user where the nearest Wetherspoons is and then showing the independent pubs around it.
 Mr Jones said he has made "a little bit of money" from carrying ads on it, and has received £200 in donations from supporters.
title: Neverspoons是一款帮助识别小型独立酒吧的应用程序，在推出的第一周就达到了1.8万次下载量。
title_en: Neverspoons, the app that helps identify small independent pubs, reaches 18,000 downloads in its first week.

note:
看家狗的粉丝可能错过了该系列的第一部续集，现在他们没有借口在隔离中再次看这款游戏。育碧周一宣布，将免费赠送Watch Dog 2，此前一个赠品故障使粉丝更难获得比最初预期的拷贝。
周日的育碧前进(Ubisoft Forward)活动对这家视频游戏公司来说是一个展示今年秋天即将推出的游戏的机会，重点是第一次(官方)看到了Far Cry 6。这次活动还让我们看到了看门狗军团及其战斗系统，它的特点是任何NPC都可以玩的游戏。
为了庆祝新的发布，育碧取笑了免费的Watch Dogs2，如果粉丝们收看了这一活动并登录到他们的育碧账户上就可以认领这款游戏。但根据一些在线粉丝的说法，他们很难登录，而且有密码问题。
别担心！即使你们无法成功登录，我们也会向你们所有人发放奖励。坐下来欣赏这场表演吧！
周一，育碧的支持账号更新了粉丝，称这款游戏将向粉丝开放--无论他们是否收看--直到7月15日。他们提供了一个注册和登录以获取代码的链接，希望在此过程中不会遇到任何问题。对于一家希望粉丝们准备好迎接今年秋天更多Watch Dog的公司来说，这是一个很好的姿态，而且免费重返生态系统也是一个很好的开始。
note_en:
Watch Dogs fans who may have missed the first sequel to the franchise now have no excuse to give the game a second look in quarantine. Ubisoft announced on Monday that it will give away  Watch Dogs 2 for free after a giveaway glitch made it harder for fans to get copies than they initially intended.
 Sunday’s Ubisoft Forward event was a chance for the video game company to  show off what’s in the pipeline this fall, highlighted by a first (official) look at  Far Cry 6. The event also gave us a look at   Watch Dogs Legion and its combat system, featuring gameplay where any NPC is playable.
 To celebrate the new release, Ubisoft had teased free copies of  Watch Dogs 2 if fans tuned into the event and logged into their Ubisoft accounts to claim the game. But according to a number of fans online, they struggled to get logged in and had password troubles.
 Don't worry! We'll be giving out the rewards to all of you, even if you were unable to log in successfully. Sit back and enjoy the show!
 On Monday, Ubisoft’s support account updated fans to say the game will be available for fans — whether they tuned in or not — until July 15. They included  a link to register and log in to snag the code, and hopefully not encounter any problems along the way. It’s a nice gesture from a company hoping fans are ready for a lot more Watch Dogs this fall, and getting back into the ecosystem for free is as good a way as any to start.
title: 育碧在经历了一次免费故障后，将给所有人提供“看门狗2”(Watch Dog 2)。
title_en: Ubisoft Is Giving Everyone 'Watch Dogs 2' After a Giveaway Glitch

note:
一项研究揭示了这种世界上最大的翱翔的鸟是如何有效地利用气流在高空停留数小时而不拍打它的翅膀的。
安第斯秃鹰有3米(10英尺)的翼展，重达15公斤(33磅)，是世界上最重的翱翔鸟类。
一组科学家首次在巴塔哥尼亚的八只秃鹰身上绑上他们称之为“每日日记”的记录设备，记录每只秃鹰超过250小时的飞行时间。
令人难以置信的是，这些鸟只有1%的时间在高空拍打翅膀，大部分是在起飞期间。一只鸟飞行了五个多小时，飞行了超过100英里(160公里)，没有拍打它的翅膀。
威尔士斯旺西大学的研究合著者、生物学家艾米丽·谢泼德教授说：“秃鹰是专业的飞行员，但我们只是没想到它们会这么专业。”
这项研究结果周一发表在“美国国家科学院院刊”(Proceases Of The National Academy Of Sciences)上。
斯坦福大学(Stanford University)鸟类飞行专家大卫·兰廷克(David Lentink)没有参与这项研究，他说：“他们几乎从不拍打翅膀，只是翱翔，这一发现令人振奋。”
对于鸟类来说，天空不是空无一人的，而是一片具有无形特征的景观：阵风，上升的暖气流，以及被山脉等地面特征推动向上的气流。
学习乘坐气流可以让一些人在长距离旅行的同时最大限度地减少拍打翅膀的消耗。
研究飞行动物的科学家通常认为飞行有两种类型：扑翼飞行和翱翔飞行。蒙大拿大学(University Of Montana)的鸟类飞行专家布雷特·托巴尔斯克(Bret Tobalske)说，这种差异可以比作兜售自行车上山和滑行下坡。他没有参与这项研究。
先前的研究表明，白鹳和鱼鹰的羽翼分别占它们陆上迁徙飞行的17%和25%。
阿根廷科马休国立大学(National University Of Comahue)的研究合著者、生物学家塞尔吉奥·兰贝图奇(Sergio Lambertucci)表示，安第斯秃鹰在翱翔方面的专业知识对它的清道夫生活方式至关重要，这种生活方式需要每天绕着高山几个小时寻找腐肉。
他说：“当你看到秃鹰盘旋时，它们是在利用这些热隆起”，也就是不断上升的暖空气阵风。
找回它们并不那么容易。兰贝图奇说：“有时这些设备会掉到安第斯山脉中间巨大悬崖上的巢穴里，我们需要三天时间才能到达那里。”
note_en:
A study sheds light on just how efficiently the world’s largest soaring bird rides air currents to stay aloft for hours without flapping its wings.
 The Andean condor has a 3-metre (10ft) wingspan and weighs up to 15kg (33lbs), making it the world’s heaviest soaring bird.
 For the first time, a team of scientists strapped recording equipment they called “daily diaries” to eight condors in Patagonia to record each wingbeat over more than 250 hours of flight time.
 Incredibly, the birds spent just 1% of their time aloft flapping their wings, mostly during takeoff. One bird flew more than five hours, covering more than 100 miles (160km), without flapping its wings.
 “Condors are expert pilots but we just had not expected they would be quite so expert,” said Prof Emily Shepard, a study co-author and biologist at Swansea University in Wales.
 The results were published on Monday in the journal Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.
 “The finding that they basically almost never beat their wings and just soar is mind-blowing,” said David Lentink, an expert in bird flight at Stanford University, who was not involved in the research.
 To birds, the sky is not empty but a landscape of invisible features: wind gusts, currents of warm rising air and streams of air pushed upward by ground features such as mountains.
 Learning to ride air currents allows some to travel long distances while minimising the exertion of beating their wings.
 Scientists who study flying animals generally consider two types of flight: flapping flight and soaring flight. The difference can be likened to peddling a bicycle uphill versus coasting downhill, said Bret Tobalske, a bird flight expert at the University of Montana, who was not involved in the study.
 Previous studies have shown that white storks and osprey flap for 17% and 25% of their overland migratory flights, respectively.
  The Andean condor’s expertise at soaring is essential for its scavenger lifestyle, which requires hours a day of circling high mountains looking for a meal of carrion, said Sergio Lambertucci, a study co-author and biologist at the National University of Comahue in  Argentina.
 “When you see condors circling, they are taking advantage of those thermal uplifts”, or rising gusts of warm air, he said.
  Retrieving them was not so easy. “Sometimes the devices dropped off into nests on huge cliffs in the middle of the Andes mountains, and we needed three days just to get there,” Lambertucci said.
title: 安第斯秃鹰可以在不拍打翅膀的情况下飞行100英里
title_en: Andean condor can fly for 100 miles without flapping wings

第一代AirPods通常被认为是完美的产品。“苹果是最好的！”是普遍接受的观点。
对噪音消除功能感到兴奋的我订购了AirPods Pro，在最初的几个月里一切都很好。有一天，每当我移动我的头时，左边的AirPod就开始嗡嗡作响。稍微倾斜我的头会产生嗡嗡的噪音，根据我的头部移动的方式，噪音的强度会有所不同。
几周后，右侧的AirPod开始发出奇怪的嘎嘎声。它听起来像是有个小部件掉了下来，现在正在AirPod里弹来弹去。风一吹，它的行为也开始变得怪异起来。
几天后，轮到了左边的AirPod，现在它又开始嘎嘎作响了。
这条产品线有很大的问题，仅仅是每隔几个月就换一条新的，在财务和生态上都是不可持续的，因为它们最终会被送进垃圾填埋场。我也远不是唯一一个有这个问题的人。
note_en:
The first generation of the AirPods was generally regarded as a perfect product. “Apple at its best!” was the universally accepted opinion.
  Excited for the noise cancelling feature I ordered the AirPods Pro and for the first few months everything was fine. One day the left AirPod started buzzing every time I was moving my head. Slightly tilting my head would result in a buzzing noise with changing intensity depending on how my head moved.
  A few weeks later the right AirPod started to produce weird rattling noises. It sounded like some tiny part fell off and was now bouncing around in the AirPod. It also started to behave weird as soon as there was a bit of wind.
  Days later it was the left AirPod’s turn and it’s now rattling again.
 There’s something very wrong with this product line and just getting a new one every few months is — financially and ecologically — not sustainable as they end up in a landfill. I’m also  far from being the only one having this issue.
title: AirPods Pro“Rattlegate”

IBM's Global Technology Services has posted a job ad calling for candidates with a “minimum 12+ years’ experience in Kubernetes administration and management”.
  Which is a little odd because the  first GitHub commit for the project was made on June 7, 2014. And the feature freeze for version 1.0 was  announced on May 22, 2015.
  Sharp-minded  Reg readers will have recognised that – absent time travel – it is therefore not possible for anyone to have 12 years’ experience with Kubernetes.
  The  ad is sadly silent on just how IBM expects candidates will have found the time to accumulate a dozen years’ experience in a six-year-old project.
  At least IBM is not alone in making such a silly mistake: developer Sebastián Ramírez, who created FastAPI and Typer, recently shared a similar experience on Twitter...
  I saw a job post the other day. 👔  It required 4+ years of experience in FastAPI. 🤦  I couldn't apply as I only have 1.5+ years of experience since I created that thing. 😅  Maybe it's time to re-evaluate that "years of experience = skill level". ♻
— Sebastián Ramírez (@tiangolo)  July 11, 2020
  Ramírez’s post sparked a lively conversation about similarly bonkers job ads, and unfortunately dopey candidates for tech jobs.
  We interviewed a 28yo designer in 2012 who told us he had 17 years experience designing websites. I said, “Tim Berners-Lee doesn’t have 17 years experience designing websites.”  “Who’s Tim Berners-Lee?” he asked.  So yeah. 🤣
— lynn boyden (she/her) (@lrb23)  July 11, 2020
  If you’ve ever spotted a daft job ad, or been asked something especially silly in an interview, feel free to  write to me and maybe we’ll get some more laughs from this situation. ®
         The Register - Independent news and views for the tech community. Part of Situation Publishing
title: IBM招聘广告要求在Kubernetes有12年的工作经验-它已经6年了
title_en: IBM job ad calls for 12 years’ experience with Kubernetes – it is six years old

note:
十年前，史蒂夫·乔布斯在D8会议上坐下来接受了卡拉·斯威舍和沃尔特·莫斯伯格的采访。随之而来的是公司和产品管理方面的大师班。整个采访值得一看，但我认为有几个片段很突出。
每当有人谈论一位科技巨头时，科技界的一些人都会条件反射地予以回击：“他不是真正的工程师”，“他不是真的……”--这篇帖子将忽略所有这些。即使你非常不喜欢他，我们也可以从中吸取教训。
采访开始时，卡拉和沃尔特祝贺乔布斯，因为苹果刚刚在市值上绕过了微软。刚走出大门，乔布斯就明确表示：
乔布斯：这无关紧要……。重要的不是什么。这不是我们的任何客户购买我们产品的原因。记住这一点对我们有好处，记住我们在做什么以及为什么要这么做。
即使他只是在为群众说这句话(我不相信他是)，他也清楚地表明了他们是关于什么的。他说得很清楚，公司里会庆祝什么。这不是关于他们的市值，而是关于制造他们的客户喜欢的东西。
从字面上看，第一个问题(在讨论市值之后)是问乔布斯关于他们放弃Flash的决定，以及他们与Adobe的“战争”。光是他在Flash上的回应就让整个采访都物有所值。他从一些微妙的东西开始：
乔布斯：苹果公司并不是一家拥有世界上所有人最多资源的公司，我们的成功之道是非常仔细地选择骑什么马。
这是一个令人惊叹的答案，特别是当它紧跟在对他们市值的讨论之后。他们当时还不是万亿美元的苹果，但他们也不是无精打采的人。2010年，方兴未艾的史蒂夫·乔布斯(Steve Jobs)，他们的市值，以及围绕Mac和iPhone的光环，几乎对任何他们想要的技术人员来说都是不可抗拒的磁铁。他们本可以在瞬间将他们的劳动力翻一番，目标是做所有的事情，但你会从乔布斯那里看到一种深刻的理解，那就是仅仅增加人员并不意味着你可以做更多伟大的事情。
伟大的产品需要伟大的人和伟大的专注。世界上的每一位产品经理都会对Dieter Rams的美学表示赞同，并会谈论说“不”的重要性。几乎没有人承诺这样做(当人们开始说他们为此疯狂时，更少的人仍然坚持自己的观点)。
在这一点上，对于苹果来说，雇佣一两个团队将一般版本的Flash硬塞到他们的设备上是微不足道的。(在刚刚发布的iPad上，这似乎是一个更大的错误)。但他是清晰而坚定的。他们会打出他们认为最能塑造伟大产品的电话。
有些事情在产品中被强调，有些事情在产品中没有做得那么好，有些事情在产品中被选择根本不做，所以不同的人做出了不同的选择，如果市场告诉我们做出了错误的选择，我们听从市场的意见，我们只是经营这家公司的人，我们试图为人们创造伟大的产品，我们至少有勇气说，我们认为这不是伟大产品的一部分，我们将把它排除在外。
我们要承担责任，因为我们想为客户制造世界上最好的产品。
当苹果今天谈到“勇气”时，这已经成了一个流传的笑话，但这样逆水行舟确实需要勇气。做出选择需要勇气，而不是简单地增加一个开关，让客户做出选择。看一看很多人“想要”的特性，并呼吁将其排除在外，这是需要勇气的。(这看起来很明显，也很容易销售，但它也面临着我们在信息安全中看到的同样的反常激励问题：排除一些东西会让你立即得到响亮的反馈，但你很少(如果有的话)得到反馈说你做得对。这就是为什么人们默认什么都会投入，也是为什么我们最终只能得到通用的、平淡无奇的软件。)。
关于乔布斯给人邮寄邮件这一相对较新的现象，有一场有趣的讨论。他们在讨论一个具体的事件(史蒂夫·乔布斯在凌晨2点20分对Valleywag说：“你为什么这么痛苦？”|VentureBeat)，但我再一次认为，这是一件启发性和洞察力很强的事情。
最近，人们对平衡、健康和休息有了很大的推动。人们很快就会指出，通宵达旦会降低工作能力，实际上会增加你的出错率，但我从来没有见过人们总是做出出色的工作，除非他们也在深夜工作，从事他们相信的项目。
这很能说明问题，这位55岁的亿万富翁将在凌晨2点“准备他的演示文稿”。也许不用开夜车就可以做大事，我只是从来没有亲眼看到过，或者我自己也不能这样做。
DUG Song经常哀叹缺乏能够演示自己产品的CEO。随便10分钟的采访都会让你相信乔布斯是聪明的，但值得注意的是他对几乎每一个提出的话题的理解深度。
当谈到富士康的话题时，你会看到他滔滔不绝地讲述着外国工厂的细节，这些细节你是不会想到他会掌握的：人均自杀率，他们调查的细节。然后，他毫不费力地谈到了手机市场份额划分和Adobe产品的技术细节。再换一次手机广告，他就会点名谈论Flory-Analytics，并描述他对手机广告的憎恨，以及手机广告的问题所在。再换一次，他谈论的是浏览器份额，以及开源WebKit如何与IE抗衡(WebKit Powering Chrome在2012年超越IE)。
我毫不怀疑他有一群聪明人带领着一群聪明人，但同样清楚的是，他深入参与了最终打动客户的每一个方面。这需要痴迷，需要时间，我假设，我们能够谈论他的深度参与的主要原因之一，是因为我们已经讨论过他投入的时间“。
很明显，乔布斯很专注(而且有点紧绷)。与他一起乘坐电梯的可怕故事比比皆是，但在采访的四分之一分，他给了我们一些可口的东西。莫斯伯格一开始就说，乔布斯职业生涯的很大一部分时间都是在与微软的平台战争中度过的。乔布斯实际上并不承认这一点。
在自嘲/轻描淡写的时刻，他打趣道：“我们从未认为自己处于与微软的平台战争中，也许这就是我们失败的原因。”这是他对自己不那么严肃的一个快速闪现，这让人解除了武装。
乔布斯：我们总是在想，如何才能制造出比他们好得多的产品，我认为我们仍然是这样想的。
没有人能听到这个，并且怀疑他们的北极星是什么。
2008年(两年前)，谷歌推出了Android手机，并开始在移动领域与苹果展开竞争。斯威舍问苹果是否计划从iPhone中移除谷歌应用程序？
乔布斯：不……。我们想制造出比他们更好的产品，而且我们做到了……。
不过，他走得更远，提出了一个有趣的话题：在消费者和企业领域制造/销售产品之间的区别：
乔布斯：我们是为了制造更好的产品，而我喜欢消费市场的原因是，我一直讨厌企业市场，那就是：我们提出一个产品，我们试图告诉每个人，每个人都会投票给自己。他们回答“是”或“不是”，如果有足够多的人说“是”，我们明天就可以来上班了。你知道这是怎么回事。这真的很简单。
然而，在企业市场中，事情并不是那么简单，使用产品的人不会为自己做决定，做出这些决定的人有时会感到困惑。
我们喜欢努力为人们制造世界上最好的产品，让他们通过他们如何用钱包投票来告诉我们我们是否走上了正轨。
这一直是一种可怕的趋势，这也是为什么组织长期遭受可怕的企业软件之苦的原因。
十年后，我们知道这种情况正在改变，iPhone和iPad等设备迅速涌入企业，刺激了这种变化。在Thinkst Canary，我们是它的巨大受益者。我们建立了一个网络安全产品，供互联网上的一些大牌使用，但我们的外观和感觉都是“消费者”的。
去年，当a16z的马丁·卡萨多(Martin Casado)谈到网络安全领域好人受到青睐的最新趋势时，他提到了我们的金丝雀，并指出，虽然我们被硅谷一些最好的公司使用，但我们足够简单，让他把我们安顿在家里。
卡萨多：让你知道的每一个复杂的标志都使用这些。他们有700多个客户，但它们足够简单，你和我可以通过网络购买并安装。因此，它只是向您展示复杂的安全性正在被消费化到什么程度(并且您应该知道这一点)。
我们认为乔布斯在2010年的预测是完全正确的，并非常确信那些丑陋的企业墙正在瓦解…。
这次采访的亮点之一(对我来说)是乔布斯关于什么先来的讨论：iPhone还是iPad？在此之前，人们一直认为苹果制造了iPhone，取得了成功，然后说：“让我们再做一款，但要更大。”
乔布斯：其实我要告诉你一个秘密，实际上我是先从平板电脑开始的。
我有一个想法，就是摆脱键盘，在多点触控玻璃显示屏上打字…我问我们的家人，我们能不能想出一种可以打字的多点触控显示屏，让我可以在上面休息，大约6个月后，他们把我叫来，给我看了这个原型显示屏，非常令人惊叹。我把它给了我们的一位出色的UI人员，他几周后给我回了电话，他做了惯性滚动工作和其他一些事情。当时我们正在考虑造一部手机，当我看到橡皮筋的惯性滚动和其他一些东西时，我说：“我的天.。我们可以用这个做一部手机。“。我搁置了平板电脑项目，因为手机更重要，我们在接下来的几年里做了iPhone。当我们重新振作起来，认为下一步可以做点什么的时候，我们把平板电脑从架子上拿下来，把从手机上学到的一切都拿出来，重新开始研究平板电脑。
首先值得注意的是，他们没有尽快推出平板电脑。他们知道这是有需要的，但他们正在做这项工作，以确定他们是否可以满足这一需求。
微软的粉丝们经常会指出，他们在iPad之前有了Windows平板电脑，在iPhone之前有了Windows智能手机，但两人都必须承认，这些设备抢走了PC，并试图将其缩小到更小的外形尺寸。所以你完全可以在你的Windows Phone上运行MS Excel，如果你能用触控笔给你鼠标指针的精确度的话……。
这并不是反对提前启动和迭代的论点，但它确实提醒我们，像所有格言一样，它遗漏了一些细微差别。苹果想要一款平板电脑，他们看到了它的前景，但他们认为，在他们的MVP达到足够的V之前，还有一些问题需要解决。
我们也看到苹果坚持己见。当你要剔除一堆平庸的想法时，很容易说“我们对事情说‘不’”。对你真正想要制造的平板电脑说不(专注于你认为现在可以制造的手机)，才是你看到这个选择真正发挥出来的地方。(我在iPad上写这篇文章，而你们中的许多人都在iPhone上阅读，这是做出这些选择的下一个顺序效应)。
作为一家年轻的软件公司，我们有着相当不错的记录。我们的NPS得分一直保持在70分以上，我们的网站上有一个页面专门提供来自谈论爱金丝雀的客户的主动反馈。其中相当一部分反馈指出，人们喜欢与我们业务的其他部分进行互动：支持；管理；甚至账单！
对我们来说，这似乎是最自然的方式。我们知道市场上有无数的产品，但是有人选择了我们。我们知道，在一个充斥着人们说话的互联网上，我们的客户已经倾听了我们的声音。公司的每一个人都意识到了这一点，并对此深表感谢。
客户有选择，客户选择了我们。从这个角度看，感恩似乎是显而易见的反应，但当客户被视为帮助你“完成季度”的道具时，感恩就消失了。
乔布斯清楚地表明，随着市值的增长，这并不一定会变得乏味：
乔布斯：我有世界上最好的工作之一，我非常幸运，我感谢我们所有的客户和员工让我做我所做的事情，我每天早上都能进来工作，我可以和我一生中遇到过的一些最棒、最聪明、最忠诚的人呆在一起，我们一起在我见过的最好的沙盒里玩耍，努力为人们创造伟大的产品。
市场份额和利润完全重要，因为它们让我们继续做我们喜欢的事情。它们是客户喜欢我们正在建设的产品的切实反馈，可以代表客户的幸福感，但对我们来说，确保自己不会违反古德哈特定律是非常重要的，而把衡量标准作为我们的实际目标。
乔布斯经常因为告诉人们他们错了而受到嘲笑。就在几个月前，苹果通过了Antenna-Gate，在那里发现他们的新iPhone在以某种方式握住时会掉线。他有句名言打趣说，人们不知道他们想要什么，直到你把它展示给他们看。但在采访中，他多次明确表示，这并不意味着永远忽视你的用户：你做了功课，做出了选择，然后你关注用户的反应。
对于一个拥有如此多非凡产品的人来说，他也不认为它们是万无一失的。在演讲中，他有几次明确表示，“我们只是在尽最大努力做我们认为人们会喜欢的事情的人类”。有人利用问答环节提醒他，在设备之间共享内容仍然很痛苦。他很快指出：
乔布斯：我们需要在这方面更加努力。我们需要做得更好。我们正在努力。
产品设计(和经营一家公司)使吉姆·柯林斯(Jim Collins)的“与的天才”脱颖而出。你需要有勇气做出选择，你需要能够倾听客户的意见并利用他们的反馈。这只是野兽的本性。
@Shreyas Doshi在他关于优秀的产品经理和优秀的产品经理之间的区别的推特上发表了一篇很棒的帖子，他说：
9/Good PM非常注重细节，确保产品达到发布时所需的质量标准。优秀的PM非常关注整个客户体验：他们知道文档、API、博客帖子、网站、支持电子邮件等也是“产品”。
-Shreyas Doshi(@Shreyas)2020年4月11日。
一位明确的苹果/乔布斯粉丝恳求他拯救电视。“你用iPhone做到了，现在省下电视吧。”乔布斯给出的答案概括了前面讨论的许多想法。
乔布斯：电视市场的问题，电视行业创新的问题是走向市场的战略。电视业基本上有一种补贴的商业模式，给每个人免费或每月10美元的机顶盒，这几乎扼杀了任何创新的机会，因为没有人愿意购买机顶盒。问Tivo，ReplayTV，Roku，问Vudu，几个月后再问Google。
索尼也试过了，松下也试过了，很多人都试过了，都失败了。
所以你能做的就是，…。回到第一步，重新设计机顶盒，使用一致的用户界面，跨越所有这些不同的功能，并以他们愿意付费的方式将其提供给消费者。
现在还没有办法做到这一点。这就是电视市场的问题所在。
我们决定了我们最想要什么产品，更好的电视还是更好的手机？嗯，手机赢了，但没有机会做得更好，因为没有办法把它推向市场。
我们更想要什么，平板电脑还是更好的电视？嗯，可能是一台平板电脑，但这并不重要，因为如果我们想要一台更好的电视，就没有办法将其推向市场。
除非有更好的策略，除非有可行的进入市场的战略，否则电视将会失败。否则你就只能再做一个TiVo了。这是根本问题。这不是技术问题，也不是视觉问题，而是根本的市场问题。
问：在手机领域，通过与运营商合作，你们能够重新制定进入市场的战略，那么与有线电视运营商合作有意义吗？
乔布斯：那么你会遇到另一个问题，没有一家有线电视运营商是全国性的。有一大批有线电视运营商，而且没有GSM标准，在美国制造一部手机，它在所有其他国家都有效，不，每个国家都有不同的标准和不同的政府批准。它非常[.]。巴尔干，但当我们说AppleTV是一种爱好时，这就是我们使用这个短语的原因。
市场上挤满了建造的产品，因为从技术上讲，它们是可以建造的。请注意，他不仅仅是在谈论打造一个好的产品，他还在考虑他们将如何储存产品，如何销售产品。他们知道这是一个有希望的领域(这就是他们保持苹果电视爱好的原因)，但他并不幻想，因为他们是苹果公司，他们可以在还不存在频道的地方开设一个频道。(当他与比尔·盖茨同台，并被问及有关计算未来的问题时，你会看到这一点的明显缓解。当盖茨愉快地谈论3D VR界面时，乔布斯则更接地气，考虑到人们习惯了什么，以及他们将如何操作它)。
在“创新堆栈”(The Innovation Stack)中，Square的联合创始人吉姆·麦凯尔维(Jim McKelvey)讲述了他是如何努力寻找导师的，直到他意识到自己不需要把自己限制在可以打电话的人身上。
乔布斯在一系列产品上制造了一系列畅销产品，并成功地产生了如此狂热的喜爱，以至于他的顶级客户几乎将“粉丝”一词定义为“粉丝”。如果我们想打造伟大的产品，仔细看看他是如何做到的，这将是明智的。
本·霍罗维茨(Ben Horrowitz)的帖子经常以当代嘻哈歌曲中的一句精选短语开始。我要走另一条路，以肯尼·罗杰(Kenny Roger)的“赌徒”(The Gambler)中的一句台词结束。
note_en:
A decade ago, Steve Jobs sat down at the D8 conference for an  interview with Kara Swisher and Walt Mossberg. What followed was a masterclass in both company and product management. The whole interview is worth watching, but I thought there were a few segments that stood out.
  Any time someone talks about a tech-titan, there’s reflexive blowback from parts of the tech community: “ He wasn’t really an engineer”, “ He wasn’t really...” - This post will ignore all of that. Even if you strongly dislike him, there are lessons to be learnt here.
  The interview starts with Kara and Walt congratulating Jobs, because Apple had just  bypassed Microsoft in Market Capitalization. Right out of the gate, Jobs makes it clear:
 Jobs:  It doesn’t matter very much... it’s not what’s important.. it’s not why any of our customers buy our products.. It’s good for us to keep that in mind, remember what we’re doing and why we doing it.
 Even if he is just saying it for the crowds (and I don’t believe he is), he’s making it clear what they are about. He’s making it clear what will be celebrated in the company. It’s not about their market-cap, it’s about making things that their customers love.
  Literally the first question (after discussing market-cap) was asking Jobs about their decision to drop Flash, and their “war with Adobe”. His response on Flash alone makes this whole interview worth it. He starts with something subtle:
  Jobs:  Apple is a company that doesn’t have the most resources of everybody in the world, and the way we’ve succeeded is by choosing what horses to ride really carefully
 This is an amazing answer, especially when it follows immediately from the discussion of their market-cap. They were not yet the Trillion Dollar Apple, but they were no slouches. The combination in 2010 of an ascendant Steve Jobs, their market-cap, and the halo around Macs and iPhones was a virtually irresistible magnet for just about any technical staff they wanted. They could have doubled their workforce in an instant and aimed to do everything, but you see a deep, deep understanding from Jobs that just adding people doesn’t mean you can do more great things.
  Great products need great people and great focus. Every product manager in the world will voice approval for the Dieter Rams aesthetic and will talk about the importance of saying “no”. Very few people commit to it (and fewer still stand by their guns when people start calling them crazy for it).
  It would have been trivial for Apple at that point to hire a team or two to shoehorn a so-so version of Flash onto their devices. (This seemed like an even bigger mistake on the iPad which had just launched). But he is clear and adamant. They are going to make calls they believe are best to shape great products.
  Some things are emphasized in a product, some things are not done as well in a product, somethings are chosen not to be done at all in a product, and so different people make different choices and if the market tells us we making the wrong choices we listen to the market, we’re just people running this company, we trying to make great products for people, and we have at least the courage of our convictions to say we don’t think this is part of what makes a great product, we going to leave it out
  We’re going to take the heat, because we want to make the best product in the world for customers.
 It’s become something of a running joke when Apple-today talks about “courage”, but it really does take courage to swim against the tide this way. It takes courage to make a choice instead of simply adding a toggle and making the customer choose. It takes courage to look at a feature that lots of people “want” and make the call to exclude it. (This seems obvious and easy to sell, but suffers from the same perverse-incentive problem we see in infosec: excluding something gets you immediate, loud feedback but you seldom, if ever, get feedback that you did this right. It’s why people default to throwing in everything, and it’s why we end up with generic, unremarkable software.)
  There was an interesting discussion on the relatively new phenomena of Jobs mailing people. They were discussing a specific event ( Steve Jobs to Valleywag at 2:20 AM: “Why are you so bitter?” | VentureBeat), but I think again, it was revealing and insightful.
  There’s been a huge push in recent times for balance, wellness and rest. People are quick to point out how burning the midnight oil translates to diminished capacity and actually increases your rate of errors, but I’ve never seen consistently great work from people unless they too, were working deep into the night on projects they believe in.
  It’s super telling that this 55 year old billionaire would be “ working on a presentation he was giving” at 02h00 in the morning. It is probably possible to do great things without burning the midnight oil, I’ve just never seen it personally, or been able to do it that way myself.
  Dug Song often laments the lack of CEO’s who can demo their own products. Any random 10 minutes of the interview should convince you that Jobs is smart, but it’s worth paying attention to the depth of his understanding on almost every topic raised.
  When the topic of Foxconn comes up you see him rattle off details on the foreign factory that you wouldn’t expect him to have at his fingertips:Suicide rates per capita, details of their investigations. He then effortlessly swaps to phone market share splits and technical details on Adobe products. Swap again into mobile-ads and he’s talking about Flurry-Analytics by name and describing his hatred of mobile ads and what’s wrong with them. Swap again and he’s talking browser share and how Open-Sourcing WebKit might make inroads against IE (WebKit powering Chrome went on to overtake IE in ( 2012)).
  I’ve no doubt he had teams of smart people driving teams of smart people, but it's also clear that he was deeply involved with every aspect that ends up touching a customer. It demands obsession, and it demands time, and I’d hypothesise that one of the main reasons we are able to talk about his depth of involvement, is because we've already discussed him "putting in the hours”.
  It’s clear that Jobs is focused (and a little tightly wound). Stories abound of terrifying elevator rides with him, but at the quarter-mark of the interview, he gives us a tasty morsel.  Mossberg begins by saying that Jobs spent a good chunk of his career fighting a platform war with Microsoft. Jobs actually doesn’t concede this.
  In a moment of self-deprecation/levity he quips “ We never saw ourselves as in a platform war with Microsoft and maybe that’s why we lost”. It’s a quick flash of him taking himself less-seriously and it’s disarming.
   Jobs:  We always thought about how can we build a much more better product than them, and I think that’s still how we think about it
 Nobody can listen to this and have doubts as to what’s their North Star.
   In 2008 (two years earlier), Google launched its Android phone and started competing with Apple on mobile. Swisher asked if Apple planned to remove Google apps from the iPhone?
  Jobs:  No... we want to build a better product than they do, and we do...
 He goes further though, and opens up an interesting topic: the difference between making/selling products in the consumer and enterprise spaces:
   Jobs:  We are about making better products, and what I love about the consumer market that I always hated about the enterprise market, is that: we come up with a product, we try to tell everybody about it and every person votes for themselves. They go yes or no, and if enough of them say yes, we get to come to work tomorrow. You know that’s how it works. It’s really simple.
 Whereas with the enterprise market, it’s not so simple, the people that use the products don’t decide for themselves, the people that make those decisions sometimes are confused.
 We love just trying to make the best products in the world for ppl and having them tell us by how they vote with their wallets whether we’re on track or not.
 This was always a horrible trend and it's why organizations have suffered for so long with horrible enterprise software.
  Ten years later, we know this is changing, and it's devices like the iPhone and iPad proliferating into the enterprise that spurred this change. At Thinkst Canary, we are huge beneficiaries of it. We build a cyber security product that’s used by some of the biggest names on the internet, but our look and feel is as “consumer” as it gets.
  When a16z’s  Martin Casado spoke about the latest trends in good guys favour in Cyber Security last year, he touched on our Canaries, and pointed out that while we are used by some of the best names in the valley, we are simple enough for him to install us at home.
  Casado:  Every sophisticated logo that you know use these. They have over 700 customers and yet they are simple enough for you and I to buy over the web and install. So it just goes to show you to what level sophisticated security is being consumerized (and you should know about it)
 We think Jobs called it absolutely right in 2010, and are pretty convinced that those ugly enterprise-walls are crumbling…
   One of the highlights of the interview (for me) was Jobs’ discussion on what came first: the iPhone or the iPad?. Until this point, it was always assumed that Apple built the iPhone, saw success, and then said: “let's make another one, but bigger”.
 Jobs:  I’ll actually tell you kind of a secret, I actually started on the tablet first.
 I had this idea of getting rid of the keyboard and  type on a multitouch glass display...and I asked our folks could we come up with a multitouch display that we could type on that I could rest my hands on and about 6 months later they called me in and showed me this prototype display and it was amazing and.. I gave it to one of our brilliant UI folks and he called me back a few weeks later and he had inertial scrolling working and a few other things. Now we were thinking of building a phone at that time and when I saw that rubber band inertial scrolling and a few of the other things, I said “my god.. we can build a phone out of this”. I put the tablet project on the shelf because the phone was more important and we took the next several years and did the iPhone.and when we got our wind back, and thought we could take on something next, we took the tablet off the shelf, took everything we learned from the phone, and went back to work on the tablet.
   The first thing worth noting is that they didn’t rush out a tablet as soon as they could. They knew there was a need, but they were doing the work to figure out if they could fill that need.
  Microsoft fans will often point out how they had a Windows tablet before an iPad, and a Windows smartphone before an iPhone, but both will have to admit that those devices took the PC, and attempted to shrink it down to smaller form factors. So you could totally run MS Excel on your Windows phone, if you could use a stylus to give you the pin-point accuracy of your mouse pointer...
  This is not an argument against launching early and iterating, but it does remind us that like all aphorisms, it misses some nuance. Apple wants a tablet, they see its promise, but they believe there’s a few problems that need solving before their MVP is V enough.
  We also see Apple sticking to their guns. It’s easy to say “we say ‘no’ to things” when you are striking off a bunch of mediocre ideas. Saying  no to the tablet you really want to build (to focus on the Phone you think you can build now), is where you see this choice play out for real. (That I’m writing this on an iPad while many of you are reading it on an iPhone is the next order effect of making those choices).
  We have a pretty great record as a young software company. Our NPS scores are consistently north of 70 and we have a page on our website dedicated to unsolicited feedback from customers  who talk about loving Canary. A fair bit of that feedback notes that people have loved interacting with other parts of our business: Support; Admin; even billing!
    To us, it seems like the most natural way for this to be. We know that there’s a zillion products in the marketplace, but someone chose us. We know that on an Internet filled with people saying stuff, our customers have listened to our voice. Every single person in the company is aware of this, and is deeply grateful to our customers for it.
  The customer has choices, and the customer chose us. Seen through this lens, gratitude seems like the obvious response but it disappears when customers are seen as just a prop to help you “ make the quarter”.
  Jobs makes it clear, that this doesn’t have to dull as your market-cap grows:
  Jobs:  I have one of the best jobs in the world I’m incredibly lucky I thank all of our customers and employees for letting me do what I do, I get to come in and work every morning and I get to hang around some of the most wonderful, brightest committed people I’ve ever met in my life and together we get to play in the best sandbox I’ve ever seen and try to build great products for people.
 Market-share, and profit totally matter because they let us go on doing what we love. They are a tangible feedback of customers liking what we are building and can be a proxy for customer happiness, but it's super important for us to make sure we don’t fall foul of Goodhart's law, making the measure our actual target instead.
   Jobs was often pilloried for telling people they were wrong. Just months earlier Apple went through Antenna-Gate where it was discovered that their new iPhone dropped calls when gripped a certain way. He famously quipped that people didn’t know what they wanted till you showed it to them. But several times during the interview he makes it clear that this doesn’t translate to ignoring your users forever: You do your homework, you make your choices, and then you pay attention to see how your users respond.
  For a person with so many phenomenal products under his belt, he also doesn’t assume they are infallible. A few times during the talk he makes it clear, “ we are just humans trying our best to do what we think people will love”. Someone uses the Q&A session to remind him that sharing content between devices was still painful. He’s quick to point out:
  Jobs:  we need to work harder on that. We need to do better. We’re working on it.
  Product design (and running a company) bring Jim Collins’ “ the Genius of the AND” to the fore as much as anything. You need the courage to make choices, AND you need to be able to listen to customers and use their feedback. It’s just the nature of the beast.
   In one of his great twitter threads on the difference between Good Product Managers and Great Product managers,  @Shreyas Doshi says:
  9/ Good PMs are detail-obsessed, making sure that the product meets the desired quality bar for launch. Great PMs pay this degree of attention to the entire customer experience: they know that the docs, the API, blog post, website, support emails, etc. are also “the product”.
— Shreyas Doshi (@shreyas)  April 11, 2020
 A clear Apple/Jobs fan implores him to save TV. “ You did it with the iPhone, now save TV”. Jobs gives an answer that encapsulates so many of the thoughts discussed earlier.
  Jobs:  The problem with the television market, the problem with innovation in the television industry is the go-to-market strategy. The TV industry fundamentally has a subsidized business model that gives everybody a set top box for free, or for $10 a month and that pretty much squashes any opportunity for innovation because nobody’s willing to buy a set top box. Ask Tivo, ReplayTV, Roku, ask Vudu, ask Google in a few months.
  Sony’s tried as well, Panasonic’s tried, lots of people have tried, they all failed.
  So all you can do, … is go back to square 1, redesign the set top box, with a consistent UI, across all these different functions, and get that to the consumer in a way that they are willing to pay for it.
  And right now there’s no way to do that. So that’s the problem with the TV market.
  We decided what product do we want the most, better TV or a better phone? Well the phone won out, but there was no chance to do a better TV because there was no way to get it to market.
 What do we want more, a tablet or a better TV? Well probably a tablet, but it doesn’t matter because if we wanted a better TV, there’s no way to get it to market.
  The TV is going to lose until there’s a better, until there is a viable go-to-market strategy. Otherwise you just making another TiVo. That’s the fundamental problem. It’s not a problem of technology, it’s not a problem of vision, it’s a fundamental go-to-market problem.
 Question: In the phone area u were able to recreate that go-to-market strategy by working with the carrier, so does it make sense to partner with the cable operator.
 Jobs:  Well then you run into another problem, there isn’t a cable operator that’s national. There’s a bunch of cable operators and then there’s not a GSM standard where you build a phone in the US and it works in all these other countries, no, every single country has different standards and different government approvals . It’s very [...] Balkanized, but when we say AppleTV is a hobby, that’s why we use that phrase.
 The market place is filled with products that were built because they technically could be. Notice that he isn’t just talking about building a good product, he’s considering how they will stock it, how they will distribute it. They know it's an area that has some promise (which is why they maintain their Apple-TV hobby) but he has no delusions that because they are Apple they can make a channel where one does not yet exist. (You see this in sharp relief when he shares a stage with Bill Gates and is asked questions on the future of computing. While Gates happily talks of 3d VR interfaces, Jobs is more grounded, considering what people are used to and how they will operate it).
  In “ The Innovation Stack”, Square’s co-founder Jim McKelvey talks about how he struggled to find mentors till he realised that he didn't need to confine himself to people he could call on the phone.
 Jobs produced a string of hits across a raft of products and managed to generate such maniacal love that his top customers almost define the term “fan-boys”. If we want to build great products, it would be smart to take a closer look at how he did it.
  Ben Horrowitz often starts his posts with a choice phrase from a contemporary hip-hop song. I’m going to go the other way, ending with with a line from Kenny Roger’s “ The Gambler”.
title: 史蒂夫·乔布斯大师班(十年前)
title_en: A Steve Jobs MasterClass (from a decade ago)
